# TechTree

TechTree is a source-backed human technology dependency graph. It helps users inspect what a technology depends on, what it unlocks, and what likely comes next.

Primary links:

- Live demo: https://pushme.site/techtree/demo.html
- CRISPR target trace: https://pushme.site/techtree/demo.html?field=Genome%20Editing%20%2F%20CRISPR-Cas&target=crispr_gene_editing#tech-crispr_gene_editing
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/yodakohl/techtree
- Contribution guide: https://github.com/yodakohl/techtree/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
- One-edge PR guide: https://github.com/yodakohl/techtree/blob/main/docs/ONE_EDGE_PR_GUIDE.md
- Edge review queue: https://github.com/yodakohl/techtree/blob/main/docs/EDGE_REVIEW_QUEUE.md
- Quality snapshot: https://github.com/yodakohl/techtree/blob/main/docs/QUALITY_SNAPSHOT.md

Useful local commands:

- `npm run agent:brief` prints branch status, quality metrics, and the current accuracy-risk queue.
- `npm test` validates schema, missing prerequisites, cycles, and temporal consistency.
- `npm run quality` runs duplicate, receipt, invariant, and quality snapshot checks.
- `npm run coverage` reports current technology coverage by era and branch.

Contribution preference:

- Prefer small, source-backed corrections over bulk expansion.
- The highest-value PR changes one edge, one source, and one receipt.
- Every semantic dependency edge should have a type, confidence, evidence level, note, review status, and source when available.
- Do not add generated placeholder technologies or vague process names.

Current trust snapshot:

- Technologies: 1,664
- Source-checked nodes: 500 / 1,664
- Nodes with node-level sources: 542 / 1,664
- Dependency edges with edge-level sources: 1,504 / 5,719
- Manual risk-weighted sample: 40 / 40 passed after correction
